data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Announces Agreement on Federal Reserve Hearing
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Besant has announced an agreement to advance a hearing concerning Walsh and the Federal Reserve. According to NS3.AI, the statement underscores the significance of maintaining policy continuity in relation to Federal Reserve actions. This move reflects ongoing efforts to ensure stable monetary policy amidst current economic considerations.

data.title: Abu Dhabi's International Holding Forms New Financial Services Entity
data.description: International Holding is establishing a new financial-services holding company to manage approximately 870 billion dirhams ($237 billion) in assets. Bloomberg posted on X, this development marks the latest consolidation effort by Abu Dhabi's largest publicly traded company. The move is part of a broader strategy to streamline operations and enhance efficiency within the firm. The newly formed entity will oversee a diverse range of financial assets, aiming to optimize management and drive growth. This strategic initiative reflects the company's commitment to strengthening its position in the financial sector and expanding its influence in the global market.

data.title: OPEC+ Members Consider Resuming Oil Supply Increases in April
data.description: Some members of the OPEC+ alliance are contemplating the possibility of resuming oil supply increases in April. Bloomberg posted on X, indicating that these members believe fears of an oversupply in global oil markets may be exaggerated. The alliance, which includes major oil-producing countries, has been closely monitoring market conditions to determine the appropriate level of production. This consideration comes amid ongoing discussions about balancing supply and demand in the global oil market. The decision on whether to increase supply will be influenced by various factors, including market stability and economic indicators. OPEC+ has previously adjusted production levels in response to changing market dynamics, aiming to maintain price stability and support economic recovery. The potential resumption of supply increases reflects the alliance's strategic approach to managing global oil resources.

data.title: Kalshi Collaborates with Game Point Capital to Offer Cost-Effective Hedging for Sports Teams
data.description: Kalshi has teamed up with sports insurance broker Game Point Capital to provide more affordable hedging solutions for professional sports teams' performance bonuses. According to NS3.AI, this partnership emerges amid regulatory challenges faced by Kalshi in several U.S. states, where courts have imposed bans on its sports markets, labeling them as unlicensed sports betting. Despite these regulatory hurdles, institutional investment and trading volumes in prediction markets have increased, with key regulators discussing jurisdiction and oversight responsibilities.

data.title: Opinion: $85,000 is the key watershed for this round of BTC upward movement, while $58,000-$60,000 is the ultimate support zone.
data.description: Deribit Chief Commercial Officer Jean-David Péquignot stated at the Consensus HK conference that Bitcoin's long-term upward trend has been broken, and the technical uptrend is unlikely to be repaired until the price reclaims $85,000. Recently, Bitcoin's price has mainly traded in the $60,000 to $70,000 range, a pullback of approximately 45% from its all-time high last October, and is likely to record its fourth consecutive week of decline. If Bitcoin breaks through $85,000 again, it would mean that buyers have largely absorbed the supply that previously suppressed the market, regaining market dominance. Currently, traders generally consider the $58,000 to $60,000 range to be the key ultimate support area for this correction cycle. (CoinDesk)

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Russia and Ukraine Announce Upcoming US-Led Peace Talks in Geneva
data.description: Russia and Ukraine have announced that a new series of peace talks, facilitated by the United States, is scheduled to occur in Geneva next week. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significance of these discussions in the ongoing efforts to address tensions between the two nations. The talks aim to explore potential resolutions and foster dialogue in the pursuit of peace. Both countries have expressed a commitment to engaging in constructive negotiations during this upcoming meeting.

data.title: Universal to Separate Downtown's Artist Royalty Platform, Curve
data.description: Universal Music Group has decided to separate Downtown Music Holdings' artist royalty platform, Curve. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move is part of Universal's strategy to streamline its operations and focus on its core business areas. The decision to spin off Curve comes as Universal aims to enhance its efficiency and better serve its artists by concentrating on its primary music-related activities. This strategic shift reflects the company's ongoing efforts to adapt to the evolving music industry landscape and optimize its resources for future growth.

data.title: Moderna Exceeds Revenue Expectations with Strong Covid Vaccine Demand
data.description: Moderna has surpassed its fourth-quarter revenue projections due to higher-than-anticipated demand for its Covid vaccines. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the company's financial performance driven by the continued need for vaccinations amid the ongoing pandemic. The biotech firm reported robust sales figures, reflecting the global effort to combat Covid-19 through widespread immunization. Moderna's success in meeting and exceeding revenue expectations underscores the critical role vaccines play in public health strategies worldwide. The company continues to focus on vaccine distribution and development to address the evolving challenges posed by the virus.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Gold and Silver Prices Stabilize Amid CPI Anticipation
data.description: International spot gold and silver prices have formed dual peaks, indicating a temporary stabilization following an overnight sharp decline. According to Jin10, the market is now awaiting guidance from the upcoming Consumer Price Index (CPI) data. This development suggests that traders are cautious, holding positions as they anticipate potential impacts from the CPI release. The dual peaks in pricing reflect a period of consolidation, with investors closely monitoring economic indicators for future direction.

data.title: US Treasury Secretary Bessant: Inflation may fall to near 2% by mid-year.
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessant stated that inflation may fall to near 2% by mid-2026, reiterating that GDP growth is very likely to reach 3% by 2025. (Jinshi)

data.title: AI giant invests $50 million to support "lightly regulated" candidates.
data.description: AI industry leaders, including Marc Andreessen, Ben Horowitz, and OpenAI co-founder Greg Brockman, have committed $50 million to a super PAC called "Leading the Future," aimed at helping congressional candidates who support mild regulation of AI win the November election. This strategy draws on the cryptocurrency industry's success in the 2024 election, aiming to leverage political influence to secure a more favorable regulatory environment for emerging technologies. The super PAC was formed at a time of growing voter concerns about AI potentially leading to higher energy costs and job losses. These tech leaders hope to build a network of allies in Congress supporting the AI ​​industry by supporting pro-AI candidates, reducing regulatory hurdles that could hinder the technology's development. (Bloomberg)

data.title: Forward Industries' financial report: As of the end of 2025, it held approximately 6.962 million SOL tokens.
data.description: Forward Industries, a Nasdaq-listed Solana treasury company, released its latest financial report, stating that as of December 31, 2025, it held 6,962,501 SOL tokens and generated 112,171 SOL in staking rewards. It also held approximately $25.4 million in cash and had no institutional debt. However, due to the overall decline in the crypto market, Forward Industries disclosed a $560.2 million loss and a $33 million impairment charge on digital assets in its first quarter of fiscal year 2026. (Businesswire)

data.title: Cogna Educação Experiences Professional-Driven Rally
data.description: Cogna Educação, a Brazilian education company, is witnessing a significant rally driven by professional investors. Bloomberg posted on X that this surge follows a period during the pandemic when the company's stock was popular among retail investors as a meme stock. The current momentum is attributed to strategic moves and market dynamics that have attracted institutional interest. This shift highlights a change in investor profile, with professionals now playing a key role in the company's stock performance.

data.title: Eurozone Faces Potential GDP Revision Amid Weak Industrial Output
data.description: Economist Klaus Vistesen from Pantheon Macroeconomics has highlighted concerns over the Eurozone's economic growth in the fourth quarter. According to Jin10, the GDP grew by 0.3% quarter-on-quarter, aligning with previous quarters and market expectations. However, Vistesen warns that upcoming data might reveal a significant decline in industrial production for December, alongside recent weak performance in the services sector. Despite this, the construction industry exceeded expectations during the same period. Overall, these factors pose a risk of downward revision for the GDP estimates of major economies like Germany and France, potentially bringing the Eurozone's growth closer to 0.2%.

data.title: Eurex Opens EU Bond Futures to U.S. Traders
data.description: Eurex has announced that its new European Union bond futures are now accessible to traders in the United States. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting that this move aims to broaden the reach of Eurex's financial products and enhance market participation. The introduction of these bond futures is expected to provide U.S. traders with more opportunities to engage in the European bond market, potentially increasing liquidity and trading volumes. Eurex's decision reflects a strategic effort to expand its global footprint and cater to a wider range of investors.

data.title: Aave's Revenue Model Overhaul Sparks Governance Debate
data.description: Aave's proposed changes to its revenue distribution model have sparked significant debate within its governance community. According to NS3.AI, the proposed adjustments focus on reallocating fees and revenues among stakeholders, leading to divisions among token holders. This governance dispute underscores the difficulties decentralized projects encounter in balancing protocol sustainability with stakeholder interests.

data.title: AI Leaders Aim to Influence Congress with Supportive Allies
data.description: Prominent figures in the artificial intelligence sector are strategizing to influence Congress by aligning with allies who advocate for less stringent regulations. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the industry's efforts to replicate the crypto sector's achievements in the 2024 elections. This move comes as AI continues to expand its influence across various industries, prompting discussions on regulatory measures. The initiative seeks to ensure that legislative decisions favor the growth and innovation within the AI field, mirroring the success seen in the cryptocurrency industry. As AI technology advances, stakeholders are increasingly focused on shaping policy to support its development.

data.title: U.S. Agricultural Market Expansion Raises Concerns Among Farmers
data.description: The agricultural sector, known for its stringent protections, is set to open its doors to the United States, sparking apprehension among local farmers. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the potential impact of this development on domestic agriculture. Farmers express concerns over increased competition and the potential for market disruption as U.S. agricultural products enter the market. This move is seen as part of broader efforts to enhance trade relations and economic cooperation between the two nations. However, the shift has raised questions about the future of local farming practices and the sustainability of domestic agricultural industries. As the sector adjusts to these changes, stakeholders are closely monitoring the situation to assess the long-term implications for the farming community.

data.title: U.S. Treasury Secretary Besent Discusses Metal Tariffs with Trade Representative Office
data.description: U.S. Treasury Secretary Besent communicated with the U.S. Trade Representative Office this morning regarding metal tariffs. According to Jin10, the discussion focused on whether the scope of these tariffs might be narrowed. The outcome of these talks could have implications for international trade and economic relations.

data.title: German Bonds Experience Strong Weekly Performance Amid Haven Demand
data.description: German bonds are on track for their most impressive weekly performance since April, driven by increased demand for safe-haven assets. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the surge in buying activity as investors seek stability amid global uncertainties. The bond market's positive trajectory reflects a broader trend of investors gravitating towards secure investments in times of economic volatility. This development underscores the ongoing appeal of German bonds as a reliable option for those looking to mitigate risk in their portfolios.
